comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report, 2:11 PM

For late March, it sure seems like winter. Today felt like a classic Saturday in January. The skies were blue, the cord was mint, and we were feeling good. As it got skied today, the general snow surface underneath was forgiving and edgeable, and all around, it was a great day.

Shall we do it again tomorrow, gang? I think so. The weather looks pretty similar, albeit a tad warmer, with temps in the high twenties at the base, and the low twenties up high. Wind will stay mild, with partly cloudy skies.

Groomers are holding their course and getting pretty much the same trails tonight, and adding in Sluice Chute and Headwall. I suspect the snow is going to be pretty similar to today, if not a bit softer after another night of grooming.

Enjoy a ride on any of these following lifts: West Mountain and Snubber at 8 AM. After that, it's King Pine, Superquad, DRC-B, Sawduster, Skyline, Bucksaw, Skidway, and Whiffletree at 8:30 AM. Moosecalator begins to spin at 9 AM.

For passholders with first tracks access, Superquad and Skyline will be your lifts for tomorrow. Reminder, tomorrow is the final first tracks of the season, so get it while you can.

It may be chilly, but soak up the spring vibes during the Spring DJ Series. From 12-3 today, check out DJ Flabbe at Bullwinkle's Deck, and DJ Rip Riccardo on the Beach. After that, head to Widowmaker from 3-6 PM with music by Primal Soup.

Stay stoked,
Shannon

comment.name: Uphill
comment.text:
Uphill travel is open to Spillway X-Cut via the West Mountain Route and the SuperQuad Route.

Visit the Uphill Policy page for detailed directions.

Note: All day-ticket uphill travelers must pick up their 1-day wristband prior to starting their ascent with Guest Services located in the base lodge. Your wristband must be visible at all times. For more information, visit the Uphill Policy page.

trail.name: Lower Sheer Boom
trail.comment: Please note Lower Sheer Boom refers to the section between Tote X-Cut and Peavy X-Cut

park.name: Drop Line Park
park.comment: "Fun Zone" featuring low-angle bumps, spines, rollers, and banked turns
